sutta: monks there are these 5 disadvantages from staying too long in a place what 5 many belongings and they're amazing much medicine and it's amazing many duties and things to be done and their concern 1 lives with householders and wanderers mixing with them not adverse from layman's company when 1 leaves that place 1 leaves it with regret amongst these are the 5 disadvantages from staying too long in a place that's the end of the sutta
commentary: during the buddha's time we find that there were many monasteries under the buddha and they were more like forest monasteries usually out of town and monks would stay in a monastery and after some time they would move to another monastery and take up residence there and after some time they move again to another monastery and that seemed to be the lifestyle during the buddha's time of course before the monasteries were formed monks used to live in caves and in up the hills in the valleys under trees etc but later the buddha allowed the formation of monasteries so that monks used to found it more convenient to stay in monasteries because they had all the support there and they would stay in the monasteries and then after that move from monastery to monastery the exceptions were those monks who had duties like the abbott the assistant abbott the uh sometimes the monk in charge of the store or monk in charge of the the kuti's the hats for monks in other words he would be the 1 who would assign monks to the goodies and other officers of the sangha who had responsibilities and some so some of these because of their responsibilities they did not move from monastery to monastery and those monks who did not have responsibilities especially the younger monks they would move and they were encouraged to not to stay too long in a place like because of these disadvantages the nexus is
